Title: Genome and systems biology of Melilotus albus provides insights into coumarins biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: NIL-Ma49 allele functionally active; NIL-Ma46 allele loss-of-function
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active coumarin metabolite; implicated in plant adaptation and defense
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Scopoletin-scopolin interconversion branch of simple coumarin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BGLU family; beta-glucosidase; clustered with AtBGLU21-23 in subclade 5
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes scopoletin production and increases scopolin/scopoletin levels, contributing to higher coumarin content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA; RNA-seq differential expression; qRT-PCR; phylogenetic inference; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assay with scopolin substrate; HPLC/LC-MS metabolite detection; hairy-root overexpression in M. albus
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaBGLU1 was identified within a tandem BGLU cluster linked by BSA to coumarin-content divergence between NILs. 2. The functional Ma49 allele encodes a full-length 506-aa beta-glucosidase, while the Ma46 allele carries a 2-bp insertion causing premature termination and loss of activity. 3. Recombinant MaBGLU1-Ma49 converted scopolin to scopoletin in E. coli-based enzyme assays, whereas MaBGLU1-Ma46 showed no detectable scopoletin production. 4. Overexpression of MaBGLU1 in hairy roots increased scopolin and scopoletin accumulation, supporting a positive role in coumarin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest expressed among the six MaBGLUs across tissues; higher in Ma48 and recurrent parent; qRT-PCR showed higher expression in Ma49 than Ma46; tissue-dependent expression differences reported
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in NILs with high coumarin content; differential across leaves, stems, flowers, roots, seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress; high- vs low-coumarin NIL background
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly PRJCA006363; transcriptome RNA-seq PRJNA647665; resequencing PRJNA759778; metabolomics reported in paper; BSA based on NIL polymorphism analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for breeding or engineering high-coumarin/high-scopoletin Melilotus lines; allele selection against the Ma46 loss-of-function variant may increase bioactive coumarins, but elevated coumarin may require caution depending on forage-use context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes nonsynonymous variation as the driver of enzyme activity divergence between Melilotus NILs; six tandem BGLUs expanded in M. albus subclade 5.
